what_it_does: Defines five named React structural primitives (Card, Rail, Stage, Composer, RhmPanel) that form the review studio surface: a registry-backed mockup gallery, a sandboxed iframe stage with element-level ui:// deixis, an addressed annotation/intent composer, and a locus-grounded right-hand-man chat panel.
observations: Exports five components: Card, Rail, Stage, Composer, and RhmPanel.
Composes shared kit primitives (Surface, SectionHead, Badge, EmptyState) and mounts the shared RhmChat organ.
Card binds a corpus row and emits onSelect; it carries data-ui-ref and maps platform values to Badge tones.
Rail groups corpus items by their group field and renders visible EmptyState messages for errors or empty data.
Stage renders a sandboxed iframe from /mockups/{file}?studio=1 and provides desktop/phone device toggle.
Stage implements element-level deixis through both a postMessage listener and a same-origin click delegate attached to the iframe document, with a polling fallback for robust attachment.
Click-deixis resolves any element to a registered ui:// address, nearest registered ancestor, or the whole-mockup base address, and records pointed element text/tag/html.
Composer displays the annotation thread at the current locus and exposes comment, request-change, and plan-mode generate actions.
RhmPanel shows address-help metadata and mounts RhmChat in studio mode, including a one-click screen-reading button.
The file carries data-ui-ref exclusions (e.g., ui://studio/composer) so studio chrome does not become the locus.
standouts: Stage's dual deixis mechanism: postMessage guard plus direct same-origin click delegate with polling fallback, making every element selectable without per-element authoring.
RhmPanel mounts the shared right-hand-man chat organ (RhmChat) at the ui:// locus instead of duplicating chat machinery.
Composer wires both shared annotations and build-intent minting at the locus, plus a generate follow-on gated on the open mockup.
Heavy inline architectural law comments: indicate-vs-execute, reflects-never-owns, registry-is-truth, no dev jargon on the operator face.
conventions: Uses functional React components with hooks, TypeScript props, useRef for fresh reads inside listeners, and useEffect cleanup.
Follows a ui:// locus addressing scheme and registry-is-truth data flow via AppContext API values.
Uses shared kit components for structural consistency and leaves aesthetic tokens to design-system variables.
Breaks strict typing in several places by using any for message data, corpus lookup, and annotation thread items; also disables react-hooks/exhaustive-deps in two effects.
concerns: Multiple any annotations weaken the declared contracts (MessageEvent data, corpus find result, annotation thread items).
Polling iframe contentDocument every 120ms for up to 2.4s is a robustness workaround but adds runtime overhead and possible race conditions.
Directly mutating iframe element styles for outline feedback couples structure to presentation and could conflict with mockup CSS; the try/catch silently discards styling errors.
Two useEffect blocks disable the exhaustive-deps lint rule, which may mask missing dependency issues as the code evolves.
Composer's generate button is gated only on an open mockup, not on hasLocus, and this file does not render generate errors or failure states.
RhmPanel passes a studio flag to RhmChat but does not visibly pass reviewAddress as a prop; locus grounding depends on AppContext/sendChat internals not shown here.
notes: StudioKit is the structural skeleton of the review studio: named, contract-headed React shells that bind backend registry truth, operator indication, and AI assistance to a single ui:// locus without executing changes directly.
questions: How does setReviewMockup consume the pointed element payload (text/tag/html) and where is it surfaced?
What are the exact runtime shapes of corpus items, annotations thread entries, addressHelp, and generateResult from AppContext?
How does RhmChat's studio mode differ from canvas mode, and how does it receive focus.selected for the locus?
What security model governs /mockups serving and the sandbox allow-same-origin policy?
How are mockups instrumented with data-ui-ref and is there a registry that validates ui:// addresses?
How are mockupGenerate failures or partial results surfaced to the operator?
purpose_vs_actual: matches — the file defines the named structural primitives, composes shared kit, mounts the shared RhmChat, and implements indicate-only deixis and reflects-never-owns as described in its header.
apparent_themes: Indicate, never execute — user clicks point; execution goes through a consent gate.
Backend/registry is the source of truth; the UI reflects it and never owns authoritative state.
Named primitives with token-slot contracts for design-system fill.
Operator-facing plain language, hiding raw ui:// address syntax.
Locus-centric grounding for AI assistance and feedback.
Structure-only components with no bespoke aesthetic.
intention_signals: Build a review studio where a non-developer can point at any part of a staged mockup, attach comments or change requests, and converse with an AI assistant grounded exactly at that design locus, while keeping execution behind an approval gate.
novelty: The combination of a sandboxed iframe stage with both postMessage and direct same-origin click-delegation to make every element selectable and derive meaningful ui:// loci without per-element authoring, plus treating the right-hand-man panel as the same chat organ used elsewhere rather than a separate feedback widget.
contribution: Provides a concrete, named set of studio structural primitives and the locus-binding logic (element deixis, addressed composer, RHM panel) that a fused system would keep as the review surface's skeleton.
summary_for_embedding: StudioKit.tsx exports five React structural components (Card, Rail, Stage, Composer, RhmPanel) that compose shared kit primitives and AppContext to render a registry-backed mockup gallery, a sandboxed iframe stage with element-level ui:// deixis, an addressed annotation/intent composer, and a locus-grounded right-hand-man chat panel.
intention_for_embedding: This file was intended to give the design-review studio named, contract-defined structural shells that bind backend registry truth, operator indication, and AI assistance to a single ui:// locus without executing changes directly.

symbols.does: Renders one gallery item for a reviewable mockup and emits a select event that indicates its file and ui:// address.
symbols.description: Card is a structure-only shell composing Surface and Badge; it binds a corpus row (file, title, platform, group, address) and raises onSelect when clicked. It carries data-ui-ref for document-level capture and maps platform values to Badge tones without painting raw address syntax.

symbols.does: Renders the grouped gallery of Cards from the backend corpus and delegates selection to AppContext.setReviewMockup.
symbols.description: Rail reads corpus and corpusErr from AppContext, groups items by their group field, and renders Cards with active state keyed to reviewMockup. It surfaces load errors and empty states visibly rather than failing silently.

symbols.does: Renders the sandboxed device stage for the chosen mockup and implements element-level ui:// deixis.
symbols.description: Stage hosts an iframe sourced from /mockups/{reviewMockup}?studio=1, toggles desktop/phone presentation, and listens for postMessage studio-deixis events. It also attaches a same-origin click delegate directly to the iframe document (with a polling fallback) so clicks on any element resolve to a registered or nearest-ancestor ui:// locus, with visual outline feedback.

symbols.does: Window message handler that validates same-origin studio-deixis postMessages and updates the review locus.
symbols.description: onMsg filters by origin, envelope type, and ui:// prefix, then calls setReviewMockup with the current file and the received address. It is registered in a useEffect that runs once and cleans up on unmount.

symbols.does: Derives a short URL-safe slug from an element's accessible name, inner text, or tag name.
symbols.description: _slug normalizes aria-label, innerText, or tagName into a lowercase hyphenated string capped at 32 characters. It is defined in Stage but appears unused in the current click-delegation logic, which instead relies on nearest registered ancestor addresses.

symbols.does: Attaches a capture-phase click listener to an iframe document to resolve any clicked element to a ui:// locus.
symbols.description: attachDeixis is idempotent via a flag; on click it chooses the element's own data-ui-ref if registered, the nearest registered ancestor if available, or the whole-mockup base address for background clicks. It also records the pointed element's text/tag/html and applies a temporary outline for visual feedback.

symbols.does: Provides the addressed-feedback input for comments, change requests, and mockup generation at the current locus.
symbols.description: Composer binds reviewAddress and annotations from AppContext, displays the annotation thread, and exposes buttons to post a comment (annotateLocus), mint a build intent (mintBuildIntent), or run a plan-mode mockup generation (mockupGenerate). It carries data-ui-ref="ui://studio/composer" so clicks inside it do not become the locus.

symbols.does: Submits the composer's trimmed text as a shared annotation at the current locus.
symbols.description: comment validates non-empty input and awaits annotateLocus, then clears the textarea. It is the handler for the comment button in Composer.

symbols.does: Submits the composer's trimmed text as a build-intent request at the current locus.
symbols.description: request validates non-empty input and awaits mintBuildIntent, then clears the textarea. It mints a plan-mode intent rather than executing a change directly.

symbols.does: Mounts the shared right-hand-man chat organ and address-help panel at the current ui:// locus.
symbols.description: RhmPanel reads reviewAddress and addressHelp from AppContext, renders a plain-language lede with a one-click screen-reading button, shows address-help metadata, and mounts RhmChat in studio mode. It grounds chat focus at the selected mockup/element locus without duplicating chat machinery.
